'''Peter Gill''' (born 14 December 1964) is an [[Australians|Australian]] former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s.

==Playing career==

Gill's position of choice was as a {{rlp|lk}} and he could also operate at {{rlp|fe}}.

Gill played for BRL club Brothers, [[New South Wales Rugby League|NSWRL]] sides the [[St. George Dragons]] for four seasons between 1988-1991 and the [[Gold Coast Chargers]].<ref>Alan Whiticker/Glen Hudson: Encyclopedia of Rugby League Players, 1995. {{ISBN|1875169571}}</ref> In the [[Super League]] he played for the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] between 1995 and 1999, and appeared in the [[1999 Challenge Cup]] Final defeat against the [[Leeds Rhinos]].

==Accolades==
He was awarded the player of the series in the [[1988 Panasonic Cup]] while playing with the [[St. George Dragons]] and in 1996 he was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]]. In 1997 he was again named in the [[Super League Dream Team]].<ref>{{cite news 
| last = Hadfield
| first = Dave
| title = Exotic Bronco enters Dreamland
| work = [[The Independent]]
| place =UK
| date = 1996-09-01
| url = https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/exotic-bronco-enters-dreamland-1361328.html
| access-date = 2010-04-24}}</ref>
